title: Podman pull in podman: Permission Denied
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

Trying to run rootless podman inside rootless podman results in permission denied errors.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Ignoring the nomad parts, as this is the source docker file and I am working on making the podman section work correctly first
```
FROM alpine:20220715
RUN apk --no-cache add nomad podman fuse-overlayfs
COPY --from=podman-driver /src/nomad-driver-podman/build/nomad-driver-podman /usr/lib/nomad/plugins
COPY storage.conf /etc/containers/storage.conf
COPY server.hcl /etc/nomad.d/server.hcl
COPY entrypoint.sh /opt/entrypoint.sh
COPY subgid /etc/subgid
COPY subuid /etc/subuid
RUN chmod +x /opt/entrypoint.sh
RUN mkdir /var/log/podman/
RUN mkdir -p /run/user/1000/podman 
RUN adduser -D nomad -u 1000
RUN chown nomad /var/lib/nomad
RUN chown nomad /var/log/podman 
RUN chown nomad -R /run/user/1000/

USER nomad
EXPOSE 4646
EXPOSE 4647
#ENTRYPOINT ["nomad"]
ENTRYPOINT ["/opt/entrypoint.sh"]
```

Then ran with
```
podman run \
		--rmi \
		--detach \
		--cidfile obj/nomad/nomad.cid \
		--publish-all \
		--security-opt label=disable \
		--device /dev/fuse \
		--cap-add=chown,sys_chroot,sys_admin,mknod \
		--volume nomad:/home/nomad/.local/share \
		--network podman1 \
		sha256:0a3d234a1a6529d7354d9e33bf3990b6aa7e4a913dd9aad99e055000b56be03d \
			agent \
				-config=/etc/nomad.d/
913047e9860329005ec663c211939a908ce81f98aef555f8fcf8e3ca5c3e9dfd
sleep 1
podman exec -it 913047e9860329005ec663c211939a908ce81f98aef555f8fcf8e3ca5c3e9dfd ash
/ $ podman pull alpine
Resolving "alpine" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 261da4162673 done  
Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:261da4162673b93e5c0e7700a3718d40bcc086dbf24b1ec9b54bca0b82300626": creating layer with ID "1b577a8fb8ce25023a0ec0a17a6dc3d6aa9cca989f75457800cb55179ee2e834": mkdir /home/nomad/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/1b577a8fb8ce25023a0ec0a17a6dc3d6aa9cca989f75457800cb55179ee2e834/diff: permission denied
```

**Describe the results you received:**

podman pull in rootless gives permission denied errors

**Describe the results you expected:**

podman pulls the container successfully

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

No

comments.body: First check if `podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map` from within the container.  Does this show a range of UIDs?

the issue is that you are creating the outer container with CAP_SYS_ADMIN so podman inside the container doesn't create a new user namespace, since CAP_SYS_ADMIN is already present, thus it runs missing CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE.

You either need to add all the capabilities, or drop CAP_SYS_ADMIN from the outer container
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
this went unanswered for long, closing it.
